zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l於1495年出生在英國林可爾史爾斯坦福時，他的父親Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire39歲，母親Alice Dycons19歲。他於1519年與Jane Heckington在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾包恩結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他在英國英格蘭盧特藍德被列為gentleman of the privy chamber & high sheriff of rutland。他去世於1553年3月19日在英國維爾爾馬爾波洛享年58歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可維斯特明斯特聖 瑪格麗特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l于1495年出生在英国林可爾史爾斯坦福时，他的父亲Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire39岁，母亲Alice Dycons19岁。他于1519年与Jane Heckington在英国英格兰林可爾史爾包恩结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他在英国英格兰盧特藍德被列为gentleman of the privy chamber & high sheriff of rutland。他去世于1553年3月19日在英国維爾爾馬爾波洛享年58岁，埋葬在英国米德萊可維斯特明斯特聖 瑪格麗特。

nameMeanings.name: Cecil
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh (Monmouthshire): from the Old Welsh personal name Seisyllt, sometimes wrongly said to derive from the Latin name Caecilius (see Sisley ). The most frequent modern spelling is a Renaissance attempt to connect the surname with Caecilius in the English pronunciation of its time. The Welsh name is more probably, but not definitely, from the Latin name Sextilius, a derivative of sextus ‘sixth’.

History: The great and powerful English Cecil family first came to prominence with David Cecil, a Monmouthshire gentleman who espoused the cause of Henry Tudor and came to court in London after the latter became king in 1485. His grandson William Cecil, Lord Burghley (1520–98), was Elizabeth I's chief adviser for 40 years, and his descendants have remained politically powerful and culturally influential in Britain ever since. They were originally minor Welsh gentry; their name is found in a variety of forms, including Sitsylt, Ceyssel, and Sisseld.

variants.name: Cissell
meanings: English (Middlesex): variant of Welsh   Cecil  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Sisley
meanings: English (Kent): from the popular Middle English female personal name Sisely (Old French Cecile, Cicile, from Latin Caecilia, feminine form of the Roman surname Caecilius, a derivative of caecus ‘blind’). This was the name of a early Christian virgin martyr of the 2nd or 3rd century, who came to be regarded as the patron saint of music. The surname is generally spelt with C- in the Middle Ages, following the trend of the personal name, and with S- in modern times. Compare Cecil and see also Sise , Sisson .

nameMeanings.name: Nicholas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the post-classical Greek personal name Nikolaos, derived from nikē ‘victory’ + laos ‘people’. The spelling with -ch- first occurred as early as the 12th century, and became firmly established at the time of the Reformation, although Nicolas is still occasionally found. St Nicholas was a 4th-century bishop of Myra in Lycia, about whom virtually nothing factual is known, although a vast body of legend grew up around him, and he became the patron saint of Greece and of Russia, as well as of children, sailors, merchants, and pawnbrokers. His feast day is 6 December , and among the many roles which legend has assigned to him is that of bringer of Christmas presents, in the guise of ‘Santa Claus’ (an alteration of the Dutch form of his name, Sinterklaas).

variants.name: Nickolas
meanings: Mainly U.S.: variant spelling of   Nicholas  , influenced by the short form   Nick .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l was born in 1495, in Stamford, Lincolnshire, England, his father, Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire, was 39 and his mother, Alice Dycons, was 19. He married Jane Heckington in 1519, in Bourne, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. His occupation is listed as gentleman of the privy chamber & high sheriff of rutland in Rutland, England, United Kingdom. He died on 19 March 1553, in Marlborough, Wiltshire, England, at the age of 58, and was buried in St Margaret, Westminster, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l est né en 1495, à Stamford, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Alice Dycons, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Jane Heckington en 1519, à Bourne, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Sa profession de son indiquée est gentleman of the privy chamber & high sheriff of rutland à Rutland, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 19 mars 1553, à Marlborough, Wiltshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 58 ans, et a été enterré à St Margaret, Westminster, Middlesex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l 1495 in Stamford, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire 39 und seine Mutter Alice Dycons 19. Er hat 1519 in Bourne, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom Jane Heckington geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. sein Beruf ist als gentleman of the privy chamber & high sheriff of rutland in Rutland, England, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb am 19. März 1553 im Alter von 58 Jahren in Marlborough, Wiltshire, England und wurde in St Margaret, Westminster, Middlesex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l è nato il nel 1495 nel Stamford, Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire, aveva 39 anni e il suo madre, Alice Dycons, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Jane Heckington nel 1519, nel Bourne, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. La sua occupazione è indicata come gentleman of the privy chamber & high sheriff of rutland nel Rutland, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì il 19 marzo 1553, nel Marlborough, Wiltshire, England, all'età di 58 anni, e fu sepolto a St Margaret, Westminster, Middlesex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil lが1495年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアスタムフォードで生まれた時，父Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshireは39歳，母Alice Dyconsは19歳であった。1519年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアボウルンでJane Heckingtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。イギリスイギリスルトランドでの職業はgentleman of the privy chamber & high sheriff of rutlandと記録されている。彼は1553年3月19日にイギリスウィルトシュアイアマルルボロウで58歳で死去し，イギリスミッドレセックスウエストミンスタースト マーガレットにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l(이)가 1495년 영국 링코실르 스타퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Dycons(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1519년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 본에서 Jane Heckington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 루틀랜드에서 gentleman of the privy chamber & high sheriff of rutland이었다. 그는 1553년 3월 19일 영국 윌츠히르 말를버루그h에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 웨스트민스터 스트 마거릿에 묻혔다.

mn.Bourne, Lincolnshire, England, United Ki: Bourne, Lindon, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l nasceu em 1495, em Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Alice Dycons, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Jane Heckington em 1519, em Bourne, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Sua profissão aparece como gentleman of the privy chamber & high sheriff of rutland em Rutland, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 19 de março de 1553, em Marlborough, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, com 58 anos, e foi sepultado em St Margaret, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l родился в 1495 г. в местности стамфорд, линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Dycons, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Jane Heckington в 1519 г. в местности бурн, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери.  Он работал по профессии gentleman of the privy chamber & high sheriff of rutland в местности рутланд, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер 19 марта 1553 г. в местности марлбороую, уилцхир, Англия в возрасте 58 лет и был похоронен в местности ст маргарет, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Nicholas Richard Cecil l nació en 1495, en Stamford, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir David Cecil, High Sheriff of Northamptonshire, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Alice Dycons, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Jane Heckington en 1519, en Bourne, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Su ocupación registrada era gentleman of the privy chamber & high sheriff of rutland en Rutland, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió el 19 de marzo de 1553, en Marlborough, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 58 años, y fue sepultado en St Margaret, Westminster, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
